arts.ttl: Predicted Stoke City starting XI vs Sheffield Wednesday as Mark Robins finds consistency
arts.sum: “I think it’s important (to have that consistency) in any case but it’s been important for us because those players have got good experience, good ability, really good qualities and they have been able to put some really solid, good performances in and, at times, some really good performances,” he said ahead of a Good Friday clash with Sheffield Wednesday (3pm). Robins might know the importance of being able to have a settled side but that was, he admits, impossible for the first few weeks after he arrived in January and there were so many players either in the treatment room or building up fitness after a treatment room stay.

arts.ttl: Stoke City sell-out is 'music to Mark Robins' ears' as he talks building blocks, fun and full voice
arts.sum: Mark Robins says the chance to go out and play in front of a near-sold out bet365 Stadium is music to his ears as he looks for a win that will pull Stoke City a long way towards Championship safety. “The club has done a great job in getting them in, pricing wise and things like that, but also excitement growing around how we’re playing, results are picking up and belief is starting to grow.

arts.ttl: Two barely believable statistics sum up Stoke City and the Championship this season
arts.sum: Thirteen teams have picked up within four points of each other since Mark Robins took charge of Stoke City in an indication of just how tight the Championship has been this season. Stoke have collected 21 points in the 17 matches since Robins took charge back of his first game on January 4, finding more consistency in performances and, with it, results as time has gone on, Robins has made his mark and he has been able to welcome key players back from the treatment room.
